arts.ttl: Frank Lampard says Coventry City must not 'wilt' under pressure at Bramall Lane
arts.sum: The Friday fixture (8pm kick-off) is Coventry ’s second of three against top four teams, and asked if he expects this to be an even tougher challenge, the head coach said: “Well, they’re all different. The Sky Blues go to South Yorkshire brimming with confidence after their comprehensive 3-0 victory over fourth placed Sunderland last time out, but the City boss knows the Blades will provide a completely different test at Bramall Lane.

arts.sum: “I’ve been watching him all week,” admitted Lampard, who knows Hamer is just one of the players his side will need to keep quiet on Friday (8pm), “so I’ve got a really good understanding of him. arts.ttl: Two back but one taken away as Frank Lampard provides mixed injury update ahead of Sheffield United
arts.sum: The good news is that attacking midfielder Jack Rudoni and skipper Ben Sheaf are fit to travel to South Yorkshire as the Sky Blues look to continue their push for the Championship play-offs against a Blades side going all out for automatic promotion to the Premier League . As for Rudoni, who is back from a dislocated shoulder that forced him to sit out the last three games, and Sheaf, who has missed the last two matchday squads with an unspecified issue, Lampard said: “I think Rudy and Sheafy should both be in the squad to travel.
